rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA DARK ET AL VS. EMILIE M CALHOUN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANTS EMILIE CALHOUN AND FIDUCIARY LAW ATTORNEYS' MOTION FOR RELIEF BECAUSE OF MISTAKE, SURPRISE, OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 473(B)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 23, 2024 line 10. DEFENDANTS EMILIE CALHOUN AND FIDUCIARY LAW ATTORNEYS' MOTION FOR RELIEF BECAUSE OF MISTAKE, SURPRISE, OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 473(B) is DENIED. The Notice of Motion filed on December 29, 2023 requests "the that the Court consider Defendant's late-filed and served index and exhibits in support of their Motion for Summary Judgment, or in the alternative, Summary Adjudication." The argument section of the Points and Authorities also discussed "index of exhibits and exhibits." Therefore, any request of relief from late filing of the separate statement (and other documents filed on December 16, 2023) raised for the first time in the reply is not properly before the Court and will not be considered. As to the late filing of the Index of Exhibits and Exhibits in support of the moving papers, moving party cites no authority for the proposition that such relief is available. Kapitanski v. Von's Grocery Co., Inc. (1983) 146 Cal.App.3d 29 and Weiss v. Chevron, U.S.A., Inc. (1988) 204 Cal.App.3d. 1094 are factually and procedurally distinguishable. The hearing on the motion for summary judgment remains on calendar. It is within the Court's discretion to consider late-filed submissions in support of timely filed Notice of Motion. It is also the Plaintiff's right to object to any filings not in procedural compliance with CCP 437c. Robinson v. Woods (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1258. The Court will determine whether it will consider the filings in conjunction with the hearing on the Motion for Summary Judgment having reviewed the opposition (if any) and reply (if any). The Court notes that the moving party failed to provide courtesy copies of its moving papers to department 501 (opposition and reply received). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
